title: crepidiastrum koshunense
intro: Crepidiastrum koshunense, also known as Koshunense Aster, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Crepidiastrum koshunense is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional East Asian medicine, such as in the form of dried roots or rhizomes.
